title: messages: sha256_hex deserialization normalizes case after validation; serializer may round-trip uppercase
description: deserialize_sha256_hex() validates that all chars are is_ascii_hexdigit() (accepts A-F as well as a-f) then calls s.to_lowercase(). This means 'AABB...'-form (64 uppercase hex) is accepted and normalized. However, the serializer (derive) writes whatever string is stored in the struct — which post-deserialization is lowercase. The issue is the validation comment says 'lowercase hex characters' but accepts uppercase. A future reviewer changing the validation to explicitly check for lowercase would silently break a client sending uppercase SHA-256 hashes. The comment is contractual; make validation match: either explicitly accept both and document it, or reject non-lowercase explicitly.
